title: Youth COP

Youth COP - SupportGoWhere
Youth COP
Share link
Aims to foster positive development and strengthen protective factors, empowering youths as crime-prevention leaders to help their communities thrive, stay safe, and build resilience.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS

About this support

Youth COP is a 3-year developmental programme that empowers students to become confident, responsible crime-prevention leaders who help keep their peers and communities safe. In partnership with Neighbourhood Police Centres and selected schools, the programme develops youths into active contributors who serve, protect, and care for their community through meaningful engagement and public education efforts.

 

Through strong collaboration with families, schools, police, and the wider community, Youth COP equips students with leadership skills, crime-prevention knowledge, and opportunities to make a positive impact. As Crime Prevention Ambassadors, participants build a strong sense of identity and self-worth, gain the competencies to navigate life’s challenges, make informed decisions, and foster safer, more caring school and community environments.

Who is this for?

The programme supports secondary school students in selected schools, with leadership potential by providing guidance, developmental opportunities, and the knowledge and skills to understand laws and their implications. Youth COP nurtures these young people into positive contributors within their schools and communities, grounded in the belief that all youths grow and thrive when supported with strong relationships, meaningful experiences, and opportunities to realise their potential.

What to expect?

Programme staff will conduct a selection interview with the nominated student, either through self-nomination or referral by school personnel.

Students’ suitability and commitment for the programme will be assessed within 2–3 weeks of referral or sign-up.

If a student is deemed unsuitable or unable to commit, they will receive a letter explaining the decision and, where appropriate, be referred to alternative support services based on the social worker’s recommendations.

How to apply?

Students can nominate themselves through the booths in school.

Students can also be nominated for the programme through school personnel (e.g. teachers).

Information last updated on 19 Dec 2025
